Protected path definition (filesystem.ts:75-81): .leviathan is listed in DANGEROUSDIRECTORIES, which triggers isDangerousFilePathToAutoEdit. 2. Safety check is bypass-immune (permissions.ts:1146-1152 / permissionSetup.ts:1144-1156): The checkPathSafetyForAutoEdit result carries decisionReason.type === 'safetyCheck'. entries.root_cause_summary: Final report identified the root-cause pattern: Fallback model path ignores user-configured custom provider endpoint Mechanism. When the streaming path hits three consecutive 529 (overloaded) errors against a custom provider behind ANTHROPICBASEURL, the retry loop in withRetry.ts (line 340) throws FallbackTriggeredError(originalModel, fallbackModel). This propagates up to query.ts (line 894) which re-issues the query using the fallback model name. The fallback model identifier originates from the codebase's internal model constants (e.g. getSmallFastModel() / getDefaultSonnetModel() in src/utils/model/model.ts). entries.root_cause_summary: Final report identified the root-cause pattern: WebFetch permission policy does not account for egress-proxy / upstream-proxy network availability. The WebFetchTool.checkPermissions() method (in WebFetchTool.ts) evaluates access purely on hostname-based permission rules — it checks isPreapprovedHost, then looks up deny/ask/allow rules keyed by domain:<hostname>. If no rule blocks the hostname, the tool proceeds. However, the actual HTTP call in WebFetchTool/utils.ts routes through axios.get, which inherits whatever proxy configuration is active (HTTPSPROXY set by getUpstreamProxyEnv() in upstreamproxy.ts, or user-set proxy env vars processed by configureGlobalAgents() in proxy.ts). If the upstream proxy relay is down, misconfigured, or its NOPROXY...

entries.root_cause_summary: Final report identified the root-cause pattern: Audited the full code-modification controller path: FileEditTool, FileWriteTool, edit-utils, atomic-write, dirty-worktree detection, worktree lifecycle, sed-edit parsing, path normalization, and query-engine recovery. Three concrete issues were identified: 1. Silent concurrent-edit loss (FileEditTool): When two parallel tool calls read the same file version, both pass the staleness guard (timestamp ≤ read-timestamp). Both write sequentially; the second silently overwrites the first's changes. The codebase acknowledges this explicitly — line 443 comment: "Please avoid async operations between here and writing to disk to preserve atomicity" — but the async mkdir + fileHistoryTrackEdit at lines 430–439 ...

entries.root_cause_summary: Final report identified the root-cause pattern: The Leviathan learning harness has zero flaky-test detection, retry, or rerun infrastructure. All 28 collected rollouts and the entire replay-promotion pipeline assume tests are perfectly deterministic. Replay comparison (replayCompare.ts) uses strict binary exact-match on exitcodes and testoutputs with no tolerance. The rollout schema seeds are null, no retry budget exists, and no test-stability metadata is recorded. A single flaky test will cause a false replay mismatch, block promotion via the p0p1regressions gate, and poison training evidence with indistinguishable noise — yet the system has no mechanism to detect or quarantine this failure mode.

entries.root_cause_summary: Final report identified the root-cause pattern: Active learning signals, session memory state, and task-level constraints can be lost or orphaned during compaction and resume flows through multiple concrete mechanisms: 1. lastSummarizedMessageId is an in-memory-only variable (sessionMemoryUtils.ts:44). It is never persisted to disk. On resume, getLastSummarizedMessageId() returns undefined, causing sessionMemoryCompact.ts:561-565 to set lastSummarizedIndex = messages.length - 1 — effectively discarding ALL post-compact messages as unreachable. The code comment acknowledges this as a 'resumed session case' but the fallback silently drops preserved-segment context rather than re-deriving the boundary from disk artifacts. 2. entries.root_cause_summary: Final report identified the root-cause pattern: The learning pipeline's candidate recovery rule for recoverycontrolfailure.badrollback prescribes only Disable feature flag <flag> as its rollback plan (heuristicTrainer.ts:57). This is a feature-flag-disable, not a state-restoration action. If a bad rollback has already corrupted state (mutated files, reset to wrong checkpoint, or left partial writes), disabling the feature flag after the fact does not undo the damage. The codebase has two stronger rollback mechanisms—a git reset --hard incident plan validator (rollbackIncidentPlan.ts:49) and a learning-activation state machine with single-step snapshot restore (learningActivationFiles.ts:179-221)—but neither is wired into the per-candidate heuristi...

entries.root_cause_summary: Final report identified the root-cause pattern: The learning command parser has three concrete argument-construction risks that can cause malformed arguments or wrong-tool invocations: 1. Tokenizer drops Windows backslash content. tokenizeArgs uses regex /"([^"])"'([^'])'(\S+)/g. The unquoted branch (\S+) matches $WORKDIR but stops at \ in some regex engines (or consumes it), so unquoted Windows paths like --out $HOMEALIAS\launch.json are silently truncated. Only --out "$HOMEALIAS\launch.json" works. The USAGE string and argumentHint show no quoted-path examples. 2. Split type / parser mismatch creates silent badargs. ParsedLearningCommandArgs['export-transcript-rollout'].split is typed as RolloutSplit, and readSplitFlag accepts traindevtestshadow...

entries.root_cause_summary: Final report found no actionable failure for this taxonomy; treat as negative/control or relabel before training: Timeout handling in the BashTool and API layer is well-structured but contains several patterns that can produce misleading success/failure evidence in training data. (1) The default bash timeout is 120s (2 min) with a configurable max of 600s (10 min) via BASHDEFAULTTIMEOUTMS / BASHMAXTIMEOUTMS env vars (src/utils/timeouts.ts:2-3). (2) When a foreground bash command exceeds the assistant-mode blocking budget (ASSISTANTBLOCKINGBUDGETMS), it is auto-backgrounded via backgroundExistingForegroundTask rather than killed — the tool result explicitly states the command 'was moved to the background' and is 'still running' (BashTool.tsx:610). ...

entries.root_cause_summary: Final report found no actionable failure for this taxonomy; treat as negative/control or relabel before training: Edit recovery and conflict handling are robust and multi-layered. The codebase implements five defense tiers against unrecoverable or misleading edit state: (1) stale-read detection with Windows mtime+content fallback, (2) transient-git-state suppression during merge/rebase/cherry-pick/revert, (3) explicit patch-application failure semantics with actionable error codes, (4) atomic temp-file-then-rename writes with non-atomic fallback, and (5) a snapshot-based file-history rewind system.
